---
title: "Functions"
---

# Main functions

The public API is intentionally small.

::: {.reference-list}
::: {.reference-item}
`path_features(path, depth = 4)`

Returns the paper-facing path coordinates.
:::

::: {.reference-item}
`classical_features(path)`

Returns classical line-profile summaries used for comparison.
:::

::: {.reference-item}
`as_spectral_path(u, F)`

Builds a two-column path matrix from an ordered coordinate and a measured
signal.
:::
:::

## Paper notation

::: {.notation-table}
::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p2`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Signed velocity--flux area.
:::
:::

::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p3u`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Location of asymmetry along the ordered coordinate.
:::
:::

::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p3F`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Whether asymmetry is concentrated in bright or faint parts of the line.
:::
:::

::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p4F`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Higher-order flux modulation.
:::
:::

::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p4T`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Twist-like higher-order structure.
:::
:::

::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p_pm`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Emission--absorption ordering.
:::
:::
:::
